sentences.text: Crazy, bro.
Afternoon, Ryan.
How are you?
Doing good, how about yourself?
I am doing good.
Last call of the day for me.
What about you?
We're getting there, yeah.
I, happy to have the call here with you.
I've got Adam.
He's the CEO of Northrock, he wanted to kind of experience the demo himself.
Sure.
Adam, how are you?
Good, Hunter, how you doing?
I'm doing well.
Where, where are you located?
I am located south of y'all.
I'm in Scottsdale, Arizona.
Nice.
How hot is it there today?
It is right now, give me a second… 1.
So, ….
Cool, cool, a cool evening, then.
It's, it's not too bad.
The, … I'm a runner, and in the mornings, at, like, 5.
30, it's been in the 80s, so it's been, it's kind of nice for, like, a 20 degree temperature change in the morning.
That's right, right, exactly.
Cool.
You, … I know you guys are located in, Utah.
Are you born and raised there?
No, I'm from SoCal, actually, I grew up in Irvine.
Oh, nice.
Orange County area, yeah.
Yeah, yeah.
I was out there for the first time for the 4th, and I definitely see the allure to,
Yes.
Yeah.
South… South Orange County is pretty awesome, especially when I was growing up there, a long, long time ago.
I'm much older than you guys.
It was, ….
still orange groves all over the place, and not as many people.
Traffic wasn't even that crazy compared to LA.
Now it's… now it's pretty full in there, but still, yeah, good spot, good spot.
Well, cool.
Nice to meet you.
Nice to connect with you again.

So I know it's, I know we kind of had the first, initial call with the group, you know, months ago, and then I think just some other initiatives kind of got in the way, so would love to kind of catch up, hear kind of what's been going on, and then I know there's some kind of re-interest in this, so just kind of walk me through, like, what's been going on.

I would say we've been, you know, pretty busy focusing on our core business of acquisitions, and we're selling… selling a few things as well, but … you know, Ryan and his team of… Talked about this tool a little bit, and, and … Wanted to, you know, consider it, and I'm happy to, just wanted to understand it a little bit better and get a sense of what it is, so… that's why he was good enough to set this up.
Sure, appreciated that, Ryan.
Thanks for the, connection.
Adam, kind of help me, understand what all Ryan and the team informed you.
That way, today, I kind of know where to fill in the gaps.

Yeah, it's just a surfacy explanation of the tool, and what it does, and how it may be able to… Speed it… you know, be… create some efficiencies for us, potentially, and in all of our modeling and underwriting, and … You know, trying to get a sense of how that all works, so….
Totally, yeah.
I'll, I'll fill you in on, pretty much everything A to Z.
So, ApartmentIQ, we have been around for the last 5 years.
Our parent company, Rentable, they do, like, ILS listings, they've been around for over a decade, so, companies well established.
we have been tracking all of this 100% publicly available market data over the last 5 years at the property level, floor plan, down to each individual unit.
So the nice thing about us is you do get, like, 5 years of seasonality, or of historicals for seasonality and trends to leverage.
Within the tool itself, there's 3 different functionalities, one of which is great for day-to-day operations.
Where you get a live data feed for your owned or managed assets in each specific comp that you're tracking for each.
So when your teams log in, they can see where their individual units and floor plans are positioned within each comp set.
That's one functionality.
Second one is great for acquisitions or new development.
You get to query our entire database, and you can create these market surveys on the fly.
Takes about 3 seconds.
All you need is the asset name or address.
You'll get a real-time market survey.
Our system will pull in what we believe to be the top 9 comps for that target property.
But the nice thing is, whether it's your asset or a target property, you and your teams are in complete control of the comp set, and you can edit those as often as you'd like.
We have two different ways to do it.
One's a map view, one is a little bit more analytical.
I'll walk you through reach, but it's super easy.
Last functionality, which might be new, Ryan, I can't remember when we spoke, if we had this, if it was up and coming, but we do have a functionality called Explore.
It's very similar to CoStar and Market Analytics.

It's gonna be all market, sub-market level information that you can drill down to the neighborhood, create custom boundaries, different radiuses, but you'll get 5 years of historicals on that market, you'll get rent growth year over year, we will include new supply, so the different stages of construction pipeline that they're in, and then you'll get demographic information for that market, just to help you understand the renter profiles.

And then something else that is new there is we'll give you a list view of all the properties, and when you dive into the data there, you'll get sales transaction, ownership information, capex, or cap rate, and, a few more different points.
So, that is… will be probably the newest release that we have.
Nice, yeah, I don't think you guys had that last time.
Yeah, so we had that, and then, about a month ago, we officially launched our revenue management software, based off of our data model.
So, we worked, directly hand-side with Willowbridge.
We have their full $200,000 portfolio on, IQ, and they basically said, hey, we're trying to get away from some of the lawsuits, could we help you guys build out a platform for revenue management?
So, we did that.
And, released it a month ago, and so I've been pretty busy with that.
Yeah.
Yep.
But, ….
So, who are your main, clients?
groups just like us?
I mean, is it generally principals that are owner-operators and, you know, looking for additional properties?
Is that the general… client base, are there other users as well?
Brokers using it?
Like, who else is in the mix?
Yeah, we do have some brokers, mostly owner-operators, third-party management companies.
Yeah.
Any other questions for me right now, off the top of your head?
Otherwise, I will, I'll share my screen.
Yeah.

I've got one question, or one of the things that I really want to understand is how easy it will be… it sounds like that you have the data, but how easy would it be to, you know, if we're underwriting a property, how easy is it going to be to understand the historical rents on that property, as well as all of its comps?
It'll be… it should be very easy.
We have an entire report on just historicals that you can date back
year, month over month, quarter over quarter, back to the last 5 years.
We'll break that down for both asking the rents and the net effective.

because I don't know if you remember this, but we do track concessions every single day as well, and apply that to a given unit and floor plan, and we present to you the asking rent, the net effective, live, and or for the historicals as well.
Yeah, I'd love to see a demo on that.
Report, specifically.
Yeah, sounds good.
Anything else for me?
I was just curious, I guess, how, and maybe this is your secret sauce, but, you know, how are you guys pulling all this data every single day?
Is it just pulling from all of these third-party websites?
Like, how does that all… how does that all work?
Yeah, good question.
So, all of our data comes from the online leasing section of the property websites, reason we do that is because those are being fed directly from, like, the internal PMS software.
So, it's just… it's mirroring exactly what a renter is going to see if they go to the website.
And to further kind of explain this, we have, like, 500 or 600 different, like, web scrapers that do this.
And since we have some skin in the game, we've gotten pretty good at developing them to get around hidden availability.
This always comes up on the calls where, you know, what if an asset isn't showing all their one-bedrooms, etc.
The reason we don't go to ILSs is because they cap availability, there's no way around it.
But if you're pulling directly from, like, sitemap, or just the online leasing section, like we do.
You can actually move, like, move-in dates, you can type in exact unit numbers or different amenity fixtures, and, our scrapers can pick up those, hidden, hidden units.
Hmm.
In the rare case that we cannot, we will make a note under that property that just says, like, hey, we believe this property is hiding units, and we don't have a workaround for it.
You can still view all the data, but at least it gives you a heads up.
What percentage of properties have that, Asterisks on them.
I don't know an exact percentage, it's very small, and most of the time, it's like a select number of, like, lease-ups.
Okay.
But not all these subs.
Yeah, I don't know the exact number.
I could double-check with the team, I just don't know off the top of my head.
Okay.
Any other questions off the bat?
Nope.
Awesome.
Fire them off, stop me.
I don't want to talk your ear off, so, I'll get them answered as best as I can.
The way it works, like I said, 3 sections.
We'll start with the My Properties.
This will be for your owned or managed assets.
Research would be acquisitions, new development.
Explore is gonna be that market, sub-market level, information as well.
So, we'll dive into it.
Each one of your properties gets its own dashboard and reporting.
To view the analytics, you just click View.
And we will start from the top.
So, the main reports of navigation here are gonna be up top.
A market survey, floor plans, each individual unit, historicals.
And then I'll have you note, the way the layout works is, we'll go over the market survey first.
There's 4 sections.
Your subject property will be highlighted here in blue.
The comps in this set are across the top here.
And what I always want to highlight is, on any report you're looking at, you can, … you can take a look at a single comp.
Or a handful of them.
You could filter everything down by bedroom, bathroom, size, rent, availability, year bill, unit style, or property class.
And the last thing here, specifically for the market survey, the left-hand side, these metrics.
If you click on Customize Metrics, there's about 40 that you could either turn on or off, depending on what's most important to you.
So, the way this works is, you send us a list of your properties, you send us a list of all the comps, we build out the shell of the data.
I touched on this earlier, but you and your teams are in complete control of your comp sets, whether it's a target property or your own.
Top right here, if we click on Edit Comps, this is how easy it is to add or remove.
Two viewpoints, right-hand side is gonna be a map view.
Again, blue pin, your property.
White pin, comps already in the set.
Black pin, comps that maybe you want to take a look at add.
You can hover over it and click Add.
Left-hand side, analytical approach.
We will grade all of these properties, give them a comp score, list them out from high to low.
That is based off of an in-house algorithm.
I always say, boots on the ground, you know your market best, this is a recommendation score that could help point you in the right direction.
It's gonna be based off of distance, year bill, total units, rent matching, amenity matching, and property class.
And again, we'll list them out from high to low.
If you want to add one of them, click Add.
All of that data and the 5 years of historicals for that property will get added to the comp set immediately.
Hunter, you said something?
I'll make sure I heard you right.

I thought you said, you know, you guys provide the property and the comps But I thought… I thought if I heard you right earlier, it's like, we just put in an address, and then you… you guys provided the comps according to these comp scores.
Which one was it there?
So, it's gonna be both.
For your own… for your own properties, we… you can either go… Okay.
our recommendations.
Right.
Or you can pick our own for our own properties, got it, okay.
So, what I was talking about earlier, research, this is, like, for acquisitions, I'll show you.
Right.
That's what I was thinking, okay.
Click in the address.
real-time market survey right here are, will pull in what we believe to be the top 9.
Got it.
Makes sense, okay.
So….
How exportable is that information?
Because we like to put all that into, like, our model, our pro forma.
Yeah.
How hard is it to transfer this to Excel?
So, like, this page right here?
the page that you were just on, where you type in a property you're looking at, and then it produces all the comps?
Yeah.
That one?
Like, if we wanted to put all those comps into an Excel spreadsheet, how much….
Yeah.
Challenge are we gonna have with that?
Not at all.
We already have it done for you.
We have pre-canned, pre-canned exports, both PDF and Excel.
You can automate these to inbox as often as you'd like.
You can also download them.
I'll just download one in the background, I'll show you here in a second what it looks like, but … I think it'll be.
Sweet.
Yeah.
Okay.

I think the other thing, too, that maybe you might like, if you guys are big fans of Excels, is, since we have been tracking all of this unit-level data over the last 5 years, if you actually click into… let me get that out of the way real quick… If you actually click into any of the property cards, I know I went fast there.
Let's just do Canada Music Grow.
There's 430 total units.
If we toggle on all units here, it can show you the exact unit mix, and then down below, you can export this data out, but this will be the status of all 430 of the units.
Floor plan, unit number, renovated or classic, square footage, status, lease applied available.
when it was first listed, days on market, gross rent, net effective, if there was a concession detail, or a concession amount attached to it, and what that detail was.
This is a great way to reconstruct, like, a rent roll, take a look at retention rates as well.
So you can export all this out, too.
What about historical data?
Yeah.
For that property.
Is that exportable as well?
Yes, they are.
So if we toggle on… if we toggle on historicals here, again, you can choose the time frame, we'll just look month over month.
The way that this is laid out is you could take a look from, like, an individual property basis.
Or you could compare your asset to, like, the comp median.

The nice thing about this, too, is we're gonna have graphs and overlays, but what I like is below each one of these metrics that we're showing you, and this is for rent, by the way, you could toggle this gross or net effective, but if you click into any of these, we'll show you the exact units that we're referencing, for those rent trends, and these can be exported as well.
This would be month over month, but this would be rent trends here, next to the concession value.
You could take a look at it as a percentage or the actual amount, and then again, below will be the raw data powering that.
We'll show you supply by the bed type.
Leasing velocity by the bed type.
And then the last thing here is gonna be your exposure leased and occupancy trends.
And again, yeah, you can export all these out, you can look at it at the property, you can look at it by bed type if you'd like.
Is that kind of what you were hoping for from, like, a historical perspective?
Yep.
Okay, awesome.
I'm jumping around, so I apologize about that, but I keep firing off the questions, and we can dive into whatever data points, I can jump around, it's not a problem.

So I touched on this earlier, you can take a look at the data live, or aggregate it back, depending on what sort of picture you're trying to paint, but from a market survey standpoint, we'll show you, … distance, property class, units, we'll link you to the Google reviews.
We'll show you rent, both asking net effective, concession details, and then something that's unique to us is we can actually show you, applications, cancels, and leases, and….
How are you getting that information?
That's one of the questions, and how do you know what they end up leasing it at, right?
Because if they just do a handshake deal and lease it, not through their portal, like, how do you get that data?
Yeah, so, the way that this works is some of our larger clients wanted us to have, like, a buffer for, like, an application period.

Obviously, some can get processed in, like, 2 to 3 days, but, our larger clients wanted it to be 7, and so the way it works is pretend Unit 1234 is on the online leasing section today, it leaves tomorrow.
We will count that as an application for 7 days.
If that unit comes back online within 7 days, we count it as a cancel.
If it does not, we will then count it as a lease.
And….
Okay.
Go ahead.
on the rate, how do you know what they leased it at?
Because a lot of times, they'll have a rate listed, and then they, you know, lower it on… on the tour or something.
So, the rate that we capture is going to be the, lowest advertised rate on the website.
If they do, to your point, some sort of, like.
shake their hand, $200 off, or a backdoor concession.
There's no way for us to report that, because again, everything we're mirroring is just getting pushed from the property management software.
Okay.
That's, like, the only caveat there.
There's really no workaround if there's, like, some sort of, like, backdoor discount.
Yeah, that makes sense.
Okay.

But I will say the one thing that is nice about this being all publicly available is we can trace everything back to the source, and then we can also click into any of the metrics, show you exactly what units we're referencing as being applied, or leased, or canceled, whatever it is.
So you can always, like, audit that if you want to.
Second section here is going to be pricing and availability.
I always say this is at a quick glance, based off of these metrics here.
You can click on any of them, sort the data high to low, low to high.
How are our studios 1s, 2s, or 3s positioned in the comp set?
The pricing will get way more granular in the next two reports, but this is just great to keep a pulse on the market, you know, based off of these metrics here.
Third section, fees and deposits.
We can point you in the right direction for any sort of ancillary fees that these properties have.
Last one would be amenities.
If it has a blue checkmark, it means that the unit has it.
And then down below here, this would just be at the community level.
Sure.
Next report here, I think you guys will like this.
This is our floor plan reporting.
So we lay out each unique floor plan in the competitive set, from square footage, low to high, and if you follow the data, we'll show you the unit mix, leased availability, and then rent trends.
And again, you can click into any of these, see exactly what units we are referencing.
I just want to show you real quick Candy Music Row.
Let's go to one bedrooms.

I think this report's great, because A it can help you price your floor plans, B, it can help you cancel out the noise, and what I mean by that is Canada Music Grow has about, like, 18 different one-bedrooms, but if you'll notice, the A4 makes up of about 11%, A9 makes up of about 11.
5%, you're most likely going to be comping out against those two floor plans.
So, it can really help you cancel out the noise and price yours accordingly.
One thing I like is if you click into any of the floor plan names in the, in the user interface, or any individual unit, we can show you the pricing history.
the gross, net effective, and the concession amount at the floor plan level or the unit.
Any sort of gap in the data like you see here just means there was none of the A9 available.
Gotcha.
Gotcha.
If this is too granular, you want the roll-ups, click on bed type, same thing, follow the data, you'll get the unit mix, least, availability, or entrance, it'll just be the averages.
Sure.
Hmm.
Last thing here, this is gonna be a massive data dump.
This is every single unit that is available in the comp set.
You can export all this out to Excel.
… The way this works.
or how I recommend to make it work is… We have the different metrics on the cards here, things like days on market, rent, price per square foot, square footage, units.
Use the filters, and it will manipulate all the data down below, just to help you get some better insights into these metrics.
So, pretend you're having a pricing call, you want to know a two-bedroom in this comp set between 1,000 to 1,200 square feet, what's it going for?
Click Apply.

You can then see the average days on market, they're being priced at $309, what the gross rents are, how many are available, how many are in your property, how many are in the comps, and then the average square foot.
And what I like is down below, we'll show you all those two bedrooms in that square footage threshold that we are referencing.
So that's kind of how the Unit 1s works.
Touched on this earlier, if you have an acquisition target.
Type in the name or address, you get a real-time market survey, you're in charge of the comps.
If you have a new development, you can create a custom property, type in the city street address, zip code, year built, total units.
If you know the unit mix, you could add all of these.
If you don't, we could suggest them for you.
And then same thought process here, you would save the property, the hypothetical property, and we would generate a real-time market survey for you on that as well.
That's Acquisitions, new development.
This is gonna be Explore.
This is all market, sub-market level information, so I'm in Scottsdale, use Phoenix.
We're looking at about… 1,000 properties, 202,000 units.
the map is interactive, so if you hover over any of the pins, we'll give you more information on that property.
3 different color pins, black is going to be existing, yellow is under construction, purple is scheduled.
But from here, what we can show you is rent performance, so 12-month trends here.
Year-over-year rent growth, and then we'll give you a 30-day pricing snapshot.
Everything's broken out by the bed type.
And what I like is for new supply, we can break out the different stages that they're in.
We can show you the deliveries quarter over quarter.
And then the last thing here is gonna be demographic information.
So this Your population, education, income, and employment.
And then I touched on this… … If we toggle on properties here, let's see….
Do you know how many markets you guys have?
Markets total?
Nationwide.
Do you know how many that is?
That you have.
Oh….
I don't know, off the top of my head.
I mean, we… yeah, we're essentially working in every single market.
Okay.
Because we'll, you know, we do some of our transactions are in, call it more secondary markets.
Yeah.
So just out of curiosity, like, if you lived, like.
one we got going on real time right now, you can say Columbia, South Carolina.
Like, let's just see, type that in, and I'd be curious to see.
Columbia, South Carolina.
Cool.
And then, the other thing I wanted to show you is… that if you, … let me go back to Phoenix real quick.
Er… I guess I could use Scottsdale.
Let's go to Scottsdale… so if we… so if you toggle on properties here, this would be a list view of all the properties.
If you dive into this, this is where we will show you, like, ownership information, management company, sales, transaction data, price availability from the bed type, estimated rent roll.
Comps for the target property.
new supply of the given area in a certain mile radius, this is 3 miles, and then demographics around this as well.
So this is, … this one here is, like, the newest release that we have in Explore.
… maybe, like, a week ago, we dropped this.
So, I think this is nice to be able to look at everything in the… in just, like, a list view, and then I just think the additional details here from the property drill down is pretty nice as well.
Yep.
Oh, and then the Excel export, let me pull it up.
Are you able to export the… all the data, market-level data, for every market in the same spreadsheet?
Or would you have to do it market by market?
The exporting from the market… I think our team is finalizing that right now.
As of right now, that's not a functionality, but I… that's… You won't be surprised that gets asked on every single call, so, it's kind of top of mind right now.
Gotcha.
So this is gonna be the Excel exports that we have for you.
So, what I like is, we'll do a property summary, we'll give you a current performance, 30, 60, 90 day outlook by the bed type.
Anymore.
Market Survey Excel, or the market survey that I walked you through, carries over into the Excel very nicely.
All we have is a visual market survey as well, and this is gonna be pretty much every key metric that you want to take a look at, and just different visuals for that.
And the thought process here was you could just export this out and have everything that you need instead of having to try to build these in-house.
Yeah.
We'll give you the bed count data, so the roll-ups, give you the floor plan level data, and then we'll give you all the raw unit level data, powering the reports.
And then one thing to note is when you're doing the exports, depending on the time that you're looking at within the application, so if you're looking at it live, all this will be live.
If you're looking at it over the past year, it'll be like that.
So, just know that any sort of filter that you have on will carry over to the export.
Gotcha.
That was… wait.
Probably a lot of information, but that essentially is ApartmentIQ.
Daily data, trying to streamline pretty much the entire process, make y'all's jobs a lot easier than trying to juggle 4 or 5 different data providers.

On that one we showed that had the, the newer stuff that had the ownership information, does it have… does it have, like, phone numbers and names, or is it just, like, the… Straight owner company name, or what is it?
How deep does that dive?
Management company, ownership group… I don't believe that it has numbers.
The other question I would add on top of that, kind of in the same vein.
A lot of times, properties are just in an LLC.
Does it just give the LLC name, or does it give, like, the true owner name?
Yeah.
… It should give the true owner name.
Yeah.
Yeah, all these I'm looking at right now, they look like all the True Runner games.
Is that what you're hoping for?
Yep.
Yeah.
I wonder, like, if you pull up one of ours, right, what would it show?
So, go to, like, Canyon View Crossing.
In Provo.
It's an Aurum, I think, technically.
Or, yeah.
Is it?
Yeah.
In Utah.
Yeah, let me share my screen.
Northwrought companies….
Rockworth.
Has the management company, too, okay.
That's, yeah, that's management company, and it's got… Northrop Companies.
Cool.
Yeah, no, … no phone numbers, though.
I've never been asked that yet.
Well, I'm a… I have a brokerage background for most of my career, so it's… we're always looking for phone numbers to cold call.
But, yeah.
From ownership side, I'm glad they're not on there, so I'm not getting the cold calls.
Yeah, yeah.
So, I don't need those anymore.
So, that's cool.
Interesting.
Okay.

I would say, I think the nice thing, too, is if there's any major takeaways from this, is that, we are a startup, technically, so I think the cool thing… I came over here from RealPage, I think the coolest thing….
Oh, yeah.

The coolest thing about working here is, if you partner with us, you do get access to our… directly to our CEO and product team, so if there are, like, certain, like, reporting or things you wish were in the product, pretty much everything that I showed you today came from customers that we have signed on over the last 2-3 years.
Right.
That's what I… that's what I kind of like, is, like, seeing this thing get built kind of from the ground up, from just customer requests.
Sure.
Just keep that in mind.
It's cool.
Okay.
Really helpful, no, it's, … Appreciate you spending the time.
It's definitely educational on my end, and I can see a lot of value there, so… makes a lot of sense.
And Ryan, you probably already know this, but Hunter, remind me of, pricing, and… What sort of friends and family discount you'd give us, and all that stuff.
Totally, yeah.
So, we typically do annual contracts.
I think, from a previous conversation with Ryan, I had quoted you guys, I think it was either $250 or $300 per license, and, I got that down to $200 per license.
Right.
Is that for a minimum number of seats, or is there a threshold you have to hit to get that?
Yeah, minimum, is 5 seats.
But I think… I think that was probably right around what you were envisioning, kind of the user case, is that right?
Roughly.

Yeah, I mean, I'm just… I gotta think about this further, but just thinking out loud, there may be a scenario where we say, look, we don't need… We don't need 5, like, we could use probably… 3, you know?
I don't know, just thinking about it, like, as a way to make it a little more economical for us, … Is that kind of thing a possibility?
I would say that's above my pay grade, but if that's… if that is, the route that you want to go, just let me know, and I can run that up the chain.
Done.
Okay.
Yeah, we'll, we'll discuss a little further internally, and… See what we think, but appreciate your time.
It was very helpful.
Yeah, for sure.
Yeah, it was nice to meet both of you.
Last thing I'll leave you guys with is, as far as, like, a setup goes, usually takes 7 to 14 business days, so it's a real quick turnaround.
Wait.
That'd be great.
Awesome.
Thank you very much.
Thanks, Hunter.
We'll be in touch.
Sounds good.
Have a good day.
You too.
